body: Indonesian Securities Rating Agency has assigned an "ida" rating to PT Merdeka Battery Materials TBK's 2024 bond, citing the company's vertically integrated business activities and strong synergy with strategic partners. The rating could be raised if the company diversifies its business, including adding downstream projects, but may be lowered if revenue and profit margins fall short of projections or if nickel prices decline significantly.
title: Indonesian rating agency assigns IDA rating to PT Merdeka Battery Materials' bonds, citing vertical integration and strong partnerships.

body: The Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index (JCI) plummeted to 7,476.99 at the closing bell, down from its starting position of 7,542.43, with other indices such as LQ45, IDX30, IDX80, and IDXESGL also experiencing declines. Despite the overall downturn, some stocks in the plantation, processed foods, banking, and energy sectors showed bullish signs, including PT Wilmar Cahaya Indonesia TBK and PT Sampoerna Agro TBK, among others.
title: Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index Plummets to 7,476.99 at Closing Bell

body: PT Harum Nickel Industry has secured a USD 45 million loan facility, equivalent to IDR 681.21 billion, to support operational activities of its subsidiary PT Westrong Metal Industry, which operates a nickel smelting facility with an installed capacity of 56,000 tons of high-grade nickel matte per year. The loan, with an interest rate of SOFR+2.60% per annum, will be used for working capital and general financing purposes.
title: PT Harum Nickel Industry secures USD 45 million loan to support operational activities of its subsidiary PT Westrong Metal Industry.

body: PT Medco Energi Internasional Tbk is set to distribute an interim dividend of USD 25.89 million, or IDR 15.75 per share, to its shareholders, following approval from the board of commissioners on October 1, 2024. The dividend payment is scheduled for November 1, 2024, and is backed by the company's strong financial performance, which saw a net profit of USD 200.99 million in the first half of 2024 and retained earnings of USD 1.39 billion as of June 30, 2024.
title: Medco Energi to Distribute Interim Dividend of USD 25.89 Million or IDR 15.75 per Share
